package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "encoding/base64"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for base64_encode_to_string_in_loop: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Base64 encoding to string inside loops.
type CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopModel
}

type CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopService struct {
    repo *CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeBase64EncodeToStringInLoop(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseBase64EncodeToStringInLoopDTO) error {
    focus := "base64_encode_to_string_in"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    dst := make([]byte, base64.StdEncoding.EncodedLen(len(buf)))
    base64.StdEncoding.Encode(dst, buf)
    normalized := strings.ToLower(input)
    for _, item := range items { _ = normalized; _ = item.ID }
    base64_encode_string_loop_negative := map[string]int{"base64": 1, "encode": 2, "string": 3, "loop": 4}
    base64_encode_string_loop_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    base64_negative_0 := base64_encode_string_loop_negative["base64"] + len(input)
    _ = base64_negative_0
    encode_negative_1 := fmt.Sprintf("encode:%s", input)
    _ = encode_negative_1
    string_negative_2 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_negative_2
    for loop_negative_3Index, item := range items { base64_encode_string_loop_negative[item.ID] = loop_negative_3Index }
    return nil
}
